title: agropyron caninum subsp. latiglume
intro: Agropyron caninum subsp. latiglume, also known as the canary grass or wild ryegrass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues, thanks to its high content of fiber and other nutrients. The health benefits of Agropyron caninum subsp. latiglume include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used as a food supplement to promote digestive health and overall well-being.
